title: Orientation styles never match the rendered data attribute
reasoning: The component renders data-orientation="horizontal" or "vertical", but its Tailwind variants look for boolean attributes such as data-horizontal and group-data-vertical. Those selectors do not match data-orientation values, so the intended horizontal flex-column layout, vertical list layout, trigger width/justification, and active indicator positioning are not applied. A default horizontal tabs instance will render the tab list and panel in a row instead of stacking list above content.
reproduction: Render <Tabs><TabsList>...</TabsList><TabsContent>...</TabsContent></Tabs>. The root receives data-orientation="horizontal" but not data-horizontal, so data-horizontal:flex-col does not activate and the root remains flex-row.
recommendation: Use value selectors that match the emitted attribute, such as data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col and group-data-[orientation=vertical]/tabs:flex-col, or render matching data-horizontal/data-vertical attributes consistently.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests or visual regression coverage were provided for the tabs component layout or orientation variants.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component/render test or visual snapshot covering default horizontal tabs and vertical tabs, asserting the generated classes/selectors apply to the rendered orientation attributes.
minimumFixScope: Update orientation-dependent Tailwind variants in frontend/src/components/ui/tabs.tsx to match the actual data attribute contract.
